canonical_definition: The degree to which intermediate sub-queries or reasoning steps are logically consistent with the prior reasoning context.
papers: MGM: Global Understanding of Audience Overlap Graphs for Predicting the Factuality and the Bias of News Media

definitions.text: The degree to which intermediate sub-queries or reasoning steps are logically consistent with the prior reasoning context.
The latent ability of a model to generate arguments that are free from reasoning errors and adhere to valid logical structures.
definitions.paper: MGM: Global Understanding of Audience Overlap Graphs for Predicting the Factuality and the Bias of News Media

definitions.text: The degree to which a model's generated arguments are aligned with valid reasoning principles and the given topic and stance.
definitions.paper: MGM: Global Understanding of Audience Overlap Graphs for Predicting the Factuality and the Bias of News Media

relationships.paper: MGM: Global Understanding of Audience Overlap Graphs for Predicting the Factuality and the Bias of News Media
relationships.evidence: We view models generating logically coherent arguments as a logical alignment problem, i.e., aligning the model responses (arguments) to the given topic and stance. (Section 1)
